assertions.description: WebCodecs VideoFrame interface is exposed to the page.
VideoFrameInit / VideoFrameBufferInit accepts and reflects a numeric `rotation` member.
assertions.test: (()=>{try{const buf=new Uint8Array(4*4*4);const f=new VideoFrame(buf,{format:'RGBA',codedWidth:4,codedHeight:4,timestamp:0,rotation:90});const ok=f.rotation===90;f.close();return ok;}catch(e){return false;}})()

assertions.description: VideoFrameInit.rotation is parsed to the nearest quarter turn and normalized modulo 360.
assertions.test: (()=>{try{const base={format:'RGBA',codedWidth:4,codedHeight:3,timestamp:0};const cases=[[45,90],[-90,270],[360,0],[1,0],[135,180]];for(const [input,expected] of cases){const f=new VideoFrame(new Uint8Array(4*3*4),{...base,rotation:input});const ok=f.rotation===expected;f.close();if(!ok)return false;}return true;}catch(e){return false;}})()

assertions.description: A 90 degree VideoFrame swaps displayWidth/displayHeight relative to codedWidth/codedHeight when no explicit display size is provided.
VideoFrameInit accepts a boolean `flip` member; the value is reflected on the resulting VideoFrame.
assertions.test: (()=>{try{const buf=new Uint8Array(4*4*4);const f=new VideoFrame(buf,{format:'RGBA',codedWidth:4,codedHeight:4,timestamp:0,flip:true});const ok=f.flip===true;f.close();return ok;}catch(e){return false;}})()

assertions.description: A default VideoFrame exposes a numeric rotation getter (0 when unspecified).
assertions.test: (()=>{try{const buf=new Uint8Array(4*4*4);const f=new VideoFrame(buf,{format:'RGBA',codedWidth:4,codedHeight:4,timestamp:0});const ok=typeof f.rotation==='number'&&f.rotation===0;f.close();return ok;}catch(e){return false;}})()

assertions.description: A default VideoFrame exposes a boolean flip getter (false when unspecified).
assertions.test: (()=>{try{const buf=new Uint8Array(4*4*4);const f=new VideoFrame(buf,{format:'RGBA',codedWidth:4,codedHeight:4,timestamp:0});const ok=typeof f.flip==='boolean'&&f.flip===false;f.close();return ok;}catch(e){return false;}})()

assertions.description: VideoEncoder is exposed — the encoder consumes the rotation/flip metadata of the input frames.
EncodedVideoChunk exists — orientation metadata flows alongside encoded chunks via decoder/encoder configs.
